title: Junko Tabei: Queen of the Mountain
story: Junko Tabei reached new heights in more ways than one! Born in Miharu, Japan in 1939, she grew up to become a legendary mountain climber. Tabei's biggest claim to fame came when she conquered Mount Everest, the world's tallest peak. On September 22, she made history as the first woman ever to stand atop Everest's summit. This groundbreaking achievement inspired countless others to follow in her footsteps and chase their own sky-high dreams.
activity_question: What was Junko Tabei's biggest claim to fame?
activity_choices: She invented a new climbing gear
follow_up_question: Can you name another famous mountain besides Mount Everest?

title: MAVEN's Martian Mission Begins
story: Zoom! A spacecraft named MAVEN raced through space, heading for the Red Planet. After a long journey, it finally reached its destination on September 22, 2014. MAVEN, which stands for Mars Atmosphere and Volatile Evolution, became NASA's newest Martian explorer. Its mission? To study Mars' atmosphere and uncover secrets about the planet's past. Scientists hoped MAVEN would help explain why Mars changed from a warm, wet world to the cold, dry planet we see today.
activity_question: What does the acronym MAVEN stand for?
follow_up_question: Why do you think scientists are interested in studying Mars' atmosphere?

title: Mighty Magnet Breaks World Record
story: Zap! Scientists in China created a super-strong magnet that's breaking records. On September 22, 2024, researchers at Hefei Institutes of Physical Science built a magnet so powerful it could lift a car! This mighty magnet produces a field over 800,000 times stronger than Earth's. It's called a resistive magnet and measures 42.02 tesla. That's the new world record! Imagine what we could learn and do with such an incredible magnetic force.

activity_choices: As strong as a car magnet

story: Floating high above the waves, David Hempleman-Adams made history on September 22, 2003. He became the first person to cross the Atlantic Ocean in an open wicker basket hot air balloon. title: Lincoln's Bold Move Against Slavery
story: President Abraham Lincoln made a daring announcement on September 22, 1862. He warned the southern states fighting against the Union that if they didn't stop their rebellion by New Year's Day, he would free all enslaved people in those areas. This bold threat, called the Emancipation Proclamation, was a turning point in the Civil War. It showed Lincoln's determination to end slavery and preserve the United States.

title: Saving Ancient Wonders from Rising Waters
story: Massive stone temples were on the move! In 1968, a special ceremony marked the relocation of Egypt's Abu Simbel temples. To bring them closer to tourists

story: A curious boy named Jean-Étienne Guettard was born in Étampes, France in 1715. He grew up to become a scientist who loved exploring the land. Guettard made history on September 22 when he created the first map showing France's special rocks and landforms. His work helped people understand what was hidden beneath their feet!
activity_question: What did Jean-Étienne Guettard create?
activity_choices: The first map of France's rocks and landforms
activity_answer: The first map of France's rocks and landforms
follow_up_question: Why do you think maps of rocks and landforms are important?

title: A Groundbreaking School Opens Its Doors
story: History was made when Xavier University welcomed its first students in New Orleans. Founded in 1915, it became the first Black Catholic college in the United States. On September 22, eager learners stepped into classrooms, ready to pursue their dreams. Xavier University opened up new opportunities for education and paved the way for greater diversity in higher learning.
activity_question: What type of institution was Xavier University?
activity_choices: The first Black Catholic college in the US
The first public university in Louisiana
The largest high school in New Orleans
A cooking school for Cajun cuisine
activity_answer: The first Black Catholic college in the US
follow_up_question: Why do you think it was important to have a Black Catholic college?

title: Lights, Camera, History!
story: Popcorn and movie magic have filled the air at the Duke of York's Cinema since 1910. This special theater in Brighton opened its doors on September 22 that year. Amazingly, it's still showing films today! That makes it the oldest cinema in Britain that has never stopped operating.
activity_question: Where is the Duke of York's Cinema located?
bonus_fact: The cinema has been entertaining audiences for over a century!

title: A Theater Built for a Show
story: Lights, camera, action! In 1921, a special theater opened its doors in New York City. The Music Box Theater was built just for a musical show called The Music Box. It was like creating a fancy new home for a beloved toy. On September 22, the theater welcomed its first audience to enjoy the songs and dances of Irving Berlin's musical revue.
activity_question: What was the name of the theater that opened in 1921?
bonus_fact: The Music Box Theater is still open today and hosts Broadway shows.
